text:
Olympiacos fans flooded the streets and squares of cities in Greece into the early hours of Thursday morning to celebrate the football team’s victory over Italy’s Fiorentina in the final of the UEFA Conference League. Olympiacos is the first Greek senior team to lift a major European club trophy.

Prime Minister Kyriakos Mitsotakis and President Katerina Sakellaropoulou congratulated Olympiacos on the Wednesday-night win.

“A true legend! Olympiacos win the Conference League and make history! An amazing night for the club itself, but also for Greek football as a whole,” Mitsotakis wrote on X.

“Olympiacos at the top of Europe! Historic night for the Piraeus team and Greek football. Warm congratulations on winning the Conference League!” wrote Sakellaropoulou.

One of the main celebrations was held in Piraeus, the home town of Olympiacos, where thousands of supporters lit flares and fireworks in the city square.

The football club defeated Fiorentina 1-0 in the final of the Conference League, Greece’s first senior men’s European title in football.

Ayoub El Kaab, a 30-year-old Moroccan player for Olympiacos, scored in the 116th minute of extra time, giving the Greek team victory against Fiorentina, who were the losing finalists for a second year running. El Kaabi is the top scorer in the 2023/24 UEFA Europa Conference League with 11 goals in nine outings in the competition.

Media reported that more than 20,000 Olympiacos fans attended the game. The 2024 Europa Conference League final took place on May 29 at the AEK Arena stadium in Athens. The UEFA Europa Conference League, which will be renamed the UEFA Conference League for the 2024–25 season, is an annual football competition organised by the Union of European Football Associations.

Police had been preparing for the match for four months, holding regular meetings with UEFA and representatives of both teams. Members of the Anti-Terrorism unit and Secret Services, EYP, were inside the stadium, a police spokeswoman told SKAI TV on May 30. A total of 5,000 police officers were deployed.

A Fiorentina fan who suffered a heart attack in the stands during the match received immediate help and was taken to the hospital. The Greek public broadcaster ERT reported that some Fiorentina fans broke seats in the stadium after their team’s defeat.

title: Greek Fans Celebrate as Olympiacos Wins European Football Trophy | Balkan Insight
author: Eleni Stamatoukou
description: Country rejoices as football club Olympiakos wins Europa Conference League, beating Italy's Fiorentina 1-0 in extra time.
